world_news.headline: Middle East crisis live: cargo vessel in strait of Hormuz struck by unknown projectile, UK maritime body says
world_news.summary:
UK maritime trade agency report comes as US is keen to portray ‘Project Freedom’ as defensive rescue effort for mariners stuck in Gulf

Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, is travelling to Beijing later today for talks with his Chinese counterpart “on bilateral relations and regional and international developments”, his ministry said on its Telegram account.

While Beijing condemned the initial US and Israeli strikes on Iran which started the war in late February, China has largely adopted a posture of neutrality ever since and has urged for a diplomatic resolution to the conflict.

Continue reading...

world_news.headline: Trump accuses pope of ‘endangering a lot of Catholics’ with Iran stance
world_news.summary:
US president directs fresh criticism at pontiff days before secretary of state Marco Rubio’s visit to Vatican

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

Donald Trump has issued another verbal attack against Pope Leo, accusing the pontiff of “endangering a lot of Catholics” because “he thinks it’s fine for Iran to have a nuclear weapon”.

The remarks come two days before Marco Rubio, the US secretary of state, meets Leo at the Vatican in an effort to ease the tensions sparked by Trump’s previous broadside against the Chicago-born pontiff over his condemnation of the US-Israeli war on Iran.

Continue reading...

world_news.headline: Mauritania’s plan to close private schools sparks backlash
world_news.summary: Mauritania’s plan to shut most private primary schools and move students into free public schools is sparking backlash.

world_news.headline: US plans to hike tariffs on EU cars to 25% will hit luxury market the most
world_news.summary: US says it will hike tariffs to 25% because the EU has not complied with trade deal last year that set tariffs at 15%.

world_news.headline: Miscalculation over Strait of Hormuz could ‘bring an all-out war’
world_news.summary: Neither the US nor Iran are willing to compromise on the Strait of Hormuz, increasing the chance of miscalculation.

world_news.headline: Russian attacks kill more than 20 ahead of rival ceasefires proposed by Kyiv and Moscow
world_news.summary: Kyiv said it would begin a truce on 6 May and then "act symmetrically" after Moscow declared a pause for its Victory Day parade.

world_news.headline: US-Iran truce teeters on meltdown as stalemate takes toll on each side
world_news.summary:
Conflict appears to have reached impasse, but leaders in Tehran and Washington seem to think victory is near

The month-old ceasefire between Iran and the US appeared to be in new peril on Tuesday with a fresh barrage of Iranian missiles reported to have targeted the United Arab Emirates as US naval forces pressed ahead with efforts to reopen the strait of Hormuz.

The Iranian strike on the UAE was the second in 48 hours, and came shortly after the US defence secretary, Pete Hegseth, insisted the shaky truce which has paused the war in the Middle East was intact, despite the new increase in violence.

Continue reading...

world_news.headline: Sudan recalls Ethiopia ambassador after strikes on Khartoum
world_news.summary: Sudan has recalled its ambassador to Addis Ababa, accusing Ethiopia of being behind attacks on Khartoum’s airport.

world_news.headline: Video: Trump says Iran should ‘wave the white flag’
world_news.summary: US President Donald Trump said Iran should ‘wave the white flag of surrender’ in the war.
